GIT_INDEX_CORRUPT.title: Corrupted Git Index
GIT_INDEX_CORRUPT.summary: Your repository's Git index file is corrupted and cannot be read.
GIT_INDEX_CORRUPT.description:
## What happened?

Git detected that the index file (`.git/index`) in your repository is corrupted. The error message typically reads:

```
error: bad signature 0x00000000
fatal: index file corrupt
```

or:

```
fatal: .git/index: index file smaller than expected
```

## Why does this happen?

This is almost always caused by **cloud synchronization software** (such as OwnCloud, Nextcloud, Dropbox, or OneDrive) interfering with Git's internal files. These sync tools may:

- Overwrite the index file while Git is writing to it
- Create sync-conflict copies of Git internal files
- Partially upload or download the index file, leaving it truncated

**Your code files are safe.** Only the Git index (a cache of file states) is affected.

## How to fix it

### Option 1: Click "Rebuild Git Index" below (recommended)

This will delete the corrupted index and rebuild it automatically. Your working files are not affected.

### Option 2: Fix manually via terminal

Open a terminal in your repository folder and run:

```bash
rm .git/index
git reset
```

### Option 3: Re-clone the repository

If the problem persists, you can delete the repository folder and let Computor re-clone it on the next startup. Your latest committed code is safe on the server.

## Preventing this in the future

- **Move your workspace outside the cloud sync folder.** This is the most reliable fix.
- If you must use a synced folder, add `.git` to your sync tool's exclusion list.
- On Nextcloud/OwnCloud, right-click the `.git` folder and choose "Ignore" or add it to `.sync-exclude.lst`.
- On Dropbox, use "Selective Sync" to exclude the workspace folder.
- On OneDrive, right-click the folder and disable "Always keep on this device".

actions.label: Rebuild Git Index
actions.tooltip: Deletes the corrupted .git/index file and runs git reset to rebuild it

actions.label: Open Terminal Here
actions.tooltip: Opens a terminal in the repository directory so you can run commands manually
